title: Pabstiella pleurothalloides
intro: Pabstiella pleurothalloides, also known as the pleurothalloid Pabstiella, is a species of orchid native to the tropical regions of South America. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation and fever, due to its purported health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Pabstiella pleurothalloides include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and glycosides, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Pabstiella pleurothalloides is typically prepared as a tea or infused in hot water to make a decoction, which is used to treat respiratory issues and as a general tonic to boost immunity.
taxonomy: Pabstiella pleurothalloides is classified within the family Pleurothallidinae, a subfamily of orchids that is native to the Americas. It belongs to the genus Pabstiella, which is a small but diverse group of epiphytic orchids. The species Pabstiella pleurothalloides is further classified within the family Orchidaceae, the largest family of flowering plants. At the kingdom level, it is classified as Plantae, which includes all plant species. Further classification would include the division Magnoliophyta and the phylum Angiospermophyta.
